title: Today's Tech Brief: The Fitbit Air is a good wearable weighed down by a chatty AI "coach"
summary: A look at the Fitbit Air wearable and the rivalry between OpenAI and Anthropic
imageAlt: Image from source article for The Fitbit Air is a good wearable weighed down by a chatty AI "coach"
imageCaption: The Fitbit Air is a good wearable weighed down by a chatty AI "coach"
imageSourceTitle: The Fitbit Air is a good wearable weighed down by a chatty AI "coach"

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's tech brief. I'm looking at a couple of interesting stories in the world of technology. First up, the Fitbit Air is getting some attention, but not all of it is positive. This wearable device is great at tracking health stats, but its AI "coach" might be a bit too chatty for some users' tastes. I mean, who doesn't love a good motivational speech, but sometimes I just want to track my steps without getting a daily pep talk.

According to a report from Ars Technica, the Fitbit Air is a solid wearable, but its AI coach is a bit too eager to help. It's like having a fitness buddy who won't shut up. On the other hand, it's clear that the folks at Google are trying to make fitness more accessible and fun. Maybe I just need to learn to appreciate a little AI-powered encouragement now and then.

In other news, the rivalry between OpenAI and Anthropic is heating up. These two AI labs have been competing for workers, customers, and public attention. What's interesting is that their investors don't seem to be picking sides. It's like a game of AI chess, and I'm not sure who'll make the next move. I guess that's what happens when you're playing with some of the brightest minds in tech.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.

OpenAI and Anthropic May Be Rivals, but Investors Aren’t Picking Sides
